Trip Briefing · Occasion: Jaya’s Birthday · #R14931

Three nights on Lake Michigan

Weko Beach Campground sits on a dune over one of the best stretches of sand in southwest Michigan. We have two sites, side by side, a two-minute walk from the water.

Check-inThu, Aug 20
Check-outSun, Aug 23

👋 the lake
the reason for all of this

This one’s for Jaya.

August 14 is her birthday. The party is a beach.

Three nights on Lake Michigan, a campfire with her name on it, and the people who love her most within s’more-passing distance. She picked the lake; the lake has no idea how lucky it is.

happy birthday, Jaya 🎂 this trip is yours.

psst — tap the water, skip a stone 🪨
the commute from camp:

Down the boardwalk,
into the lake.

Sugar sand, Caribbean-looking water (seriously, look at it), and a beach that goes for miles. August water temps are as warm as Lake Michigan gets.

~2 mincamp to sand

sneak preview

What we're walking into

home for 3 nights

Base camp: sites 45 & 46

↑ the actual 45 & 46 (city's photos — that's fall, we get full summer)

These aren't stock photos. That's literally our dirt: site 45 underneath, 46 on top, posts and all. Grassy pads backed up against the woods, right next to each other.

Electric pedestals at both, so the cooler, the lights and the phone chargers all live another day. Each site has its own fire ring and picnic table. One camp kitchen, one big fire, two tents' worth of room to spread out.

It's the rustic loop, and that's the charm. Tall trees, real shade, the lake doing its thing just over the dune.

⚡ Electric at both sites 🔥 Fire rings 🧺 Picnic tables 🌲 Shaded + wooded 🏖️ Walk to beach 🚿 Showers in the park
the lay of the land

Where we're parked

the actual park map (City of Bridgman / Wightman survey). tent → sand in about 2 minutes. tap the dots ☝️
↔ swipe the map around
real talk

Know before you go

🕒
Arrive 3–6pm Thursday

Check-in opens at 3pm and the office wants everyone checked in by 6pm. Running late? Call the park: (269) 465-3406.

🚻
Bathroom intel

Our loop runs porta-potties; the real restrooms and hot showers are a short walk or bike ride away in the east and west loops. Pack shower shoes. You've camped before.

🌅
Sunsets are the event

West-facing beach means front-row sunset seats every night. Sunset hits around 8:30 in late August. Attendance is mandatory.

🥾
Dune trail + observation deck

A wooded boardwalk trail climbs the dunes behind camp, creek crossing and all, up to an observation deck over the lake. Easy morning hike.

🍦
Beach house perks

Concessions, the Sunset Room cafe, and kayak + paddleboard rentals right at the beach. Ice cream is a daily line item, not a treat.

🍷
Wine country next door

Bridgman sits in Michigan's southwest wine belt. Weko Beach Bar & Grill is in town, and a dozen wineries and breweries are within 20 minutes if we want an afternoon mission.

programming schedule

The nightly show

West-facing beach, so the sun drops straight into the lake every night. Then the moon takes over the second shift — it's waxing toward full all weekend, hanging right over the water.

🌓Thursday
8:39 pm
First-quarter moon, half lit. Campfire night one.
🌔Friday
8:38 pm
62% moon over the lake. Bring the good camera.
🌔Saturday
8:36 pm
Waxing gibbous, 72% lit. Moonlight-on-water hours.
78°Favg day · these exact dates
63°Favg night · these exact dates
low 70slake temp (its warmest)

10-year average for Aug 20–23 in Bridgman. For the record: the hottest those dates have hit is 94°F, the coolest night 50°F. Translation: pack one warm layer and zero regrets.

operation: get there

Getting there

Metro Detroit → I-94 West → Bridgman
≈ 200 miles · ≈ 3 hours

One highway, basically. I-94 west until Michigan runs out of Michigan, exit at Bridgman, five minutes to the lake.

Thursday math: check-in opens at 3pm and the office wants everyone in by 6pm. Leaving by noon = there by 3:30 with a stop. Don't be the car that makes us explain you to the ranger.

pro move: grab firewood + ice in Bridgman before the last turn.

nobody is exempt

Trip staff, now hiring

🔥
Fire Marshal

Owns the campfire. Judged solely on flame height and s'mores readiness.

🥓
Grill Sergeant

Runs the camp kitchen. Has strong opinions about hot dog doneness.

☕
Coffee Czar

First one up. The most important job on this list and it's not close.

🍫
S'mores QA

Quality assurance on every marshmallow. A burden, truly.

📸
Sunset Photographer

On duty 8:30–8:45pm nightly. The lake does the hard part.

🔊
Playlist Director

Campground volume. Quiet hours are real. Taste is mandatory.

🧊
Ice Runner

Knows where the bags are cheapest in Bridgman by day two.

🧭
Trail Boss

Leads the dune hike. Allowed to say "almost there" a maximum of twice.

claim your title in the group chat. dish duty rotates, don't even try it.

See you at the campfire.

press & hold the marshmallow. release at golden. 🔥
